body:
Owner: Alex Chen
Status: Proposed
Last Updated: 20 May 2026

**Objective:** For Q3 2026, the Lantern team will focus on driving enterprise retention by delivering critical performance improvements and enterprise-grade features.

**Theme 1: Performance for Power Users**
*Problem:* Our largest customers (e.g., Acme Co., $480k ARR) are experiencing performance degradation that puts renewals at risk.
*Initiatives:* Address query optimization back-end (LAN-1988), refactor dashboard data fetching (LAN-2031).
*Metric:* Reduce P95 dashboard load time by 50% for users with >100 assets.

**Theme 2: Enterprise Readiness**
*Problem:* We have blockers for retention (Acme) and new sales (United Health) related to specific enterprise needs.
*Initiatives:* Implement support for the new pricing & packaging model. Scope and deliver custom PDF exports (LAN-2045).
*Metric:* Mitigate Acme renewal risk, unblock $600k United Health deal.

**Theme 3: Core Workflow Polish**
*Problem:* Our query builder remains a point of friction for both new and existing users.
*Initiatives:* Targeted UX improvements to query builder flow based on Ben's v2 designs.
*Metric:* Reduce support tickets related to query construction by 25%.

We are explicitly *deprioritizing* the platform-wide Audit Log initiative for Q3. The engineering effort (est. 10-12 weeks) is a direct conflict with the Performance theme, and we are prioritizing the immediate $480k renewal risk with Acme.

title: Project Brief: Lantern Saved Views
body:
User Problem
Power users and teams who collaborate on analysis need a way to create, save, and share specific cuts of data. Today, they do this by manually re-applying a complex set of filters every time they enter the app, or by sharing screenshots. This is inefficient and error-prone.

Proposed Solution
The 'Saved Views' feature will allow a user to configure a dashboard with a specific set of global and widget-level filters and save this configuration as a unique, shareable view. Other users can then load this view to see the exact same data cut.

Business & User Impact
-   For users: Drastically reduces time-to-insight for recurring analysis. Enables more effective collaboration.
-   For business: Unlocks stickiness and enterprise readiness. Directly addresses feedback from key accounts (Acme Co.) and is a key competitive differentiator.
-   Risk: As flagged by engineering, the technical implementation for cross-source filter states is complex and may jeopardize the Q3 launch timeline.

title: Lantern Q3 Launch: Scope Trade-offs
body:
Objective: Decide on Lantern scope to ensure a successful, on-time launch by 30 June 2026.

Background: The 'Saved Views' feature, while high-value, has been identified as high-risk by Engineering (see LAN-2031, LAN-2044) and the Platform team. Estimates suggest it would delay the launch by 6-8 weeks.

Option A: Delay Launch
-   Description: Delay the entire Lantern launch to late Q3 to include the full 'Saved Views' feature.
-   Pros: Launch with a stronger competitive feature set.
-   Cons: Misses public Q2 deadline. Delays value delivery for all other Lantern features. Risks team morale and momentum. Leaves accounts like Acme waiting longer.

Option B: Phased Launch (Recommended)
-   Description: Descope 'Saved Views' from the initial launch. Launch the core Lantern workspace on 30 June. Commit to 'Saved Views' as a fast-follow, targeting Q4.
-   Pros: Hits the 30 June deadline. Delivers significant user value on time. Mitigates technical risk. Allows Engineering to architect Saved Views properly without rushing.
-   Cons: Initial launch has a smaller feature set. Requires careful stakeholder and customer messaging.

Recommendation: Pursue Option B. The risk of delay from Option A is too high. A successful, on-time launch of the core workspace will build momentum and give us a stable foundation to add Saved Views in Q4.

title: Lantern Q3 Launch - Recommended Path
body:
Priya -

Here is the trade-off doc and recommendation you asked for. Link: [helio.docs/lantern-q3-tradeoffs]

Tl;dr: I'm recommending we descope Saved Views from the initial launch to guarantee the 30 June date and mitigate technical risk.

-   Launch core Lantern workspace by 30 June.
-   Fast-follow with Saved Views in Q4.
-   Use the interim to properly architect the query layer with Eng/Platform.

This is the most responsible path to deliver value and manage risk. Let me know if you agree.

Alex

title: Acme Co. Pricing Exception - Recommendation
body:
Doc ID: PM-LAN-2026-017
Author: Alex Chen
Date: 15 May 2026

**1. Summary**
Acme Co. (C-10034, $480k ARR) has formally objected to the Q3 pricing model change, which results in an 18% ($86k) increase to their annual rate. This document outlines three potential responses and recommends a path forward.

**2. Background**
The price increase is driven by Acme's high consumption of compute-intensive custom event processing, a pattern that is 5x higher than other enterprise accounts. Their current contract renews on October 1, 2026. Sales (Layla Hassan) and CS (Diego Salas) assess the renewal as 'at-risk' if we hold firm without concessions.

**3. Options Analysis**
*   **Option 1: Hold Firm.** Communicate the value of the platform and enforce the new pricing. 
    *   Pros: Maintains pricing integrity, avoids setting a precedent.
    *   Cons: High risk of losing a $480k ARR customer and reference logo.

*   **Option 2: One-Time Service Credit.** Offer a one-time credit of $40,000 (approx. 50% of the increase) for their first year on the new plan, positioned as a 'strategic partnership investment' to help them transition. 
    *   Pros: Mitigates immediate churn risk, shows goodwill, avoids a permanent discount structure.
    *   Cons: Still erodes margin, may only delay the problem.

*   **Option 3: Grandfather Pricing.** Allow Acme to remain on the legacy pricing model for the duration of their next contract term.
    *   Pros: Guarantees renewal.
    *   Cons: Sets a major precedent for other large accounts, undermines the entire pricing initiative, significant revenue loss over time.

**4. Recommendation**
We recommend pursuing **Option 2**. It offers the best balance of retaining a strategic customer while directionally enforcing our new pricing model. The credit should be contingent on a joint 'efficiency review' where we help them optimize their usage, reducing their long-term cost burden and our own COGS.

title: Junior PM Interview Guide - Lantern Team
body:
Hiring Profile: Junior PM, Lantern Team

We are looking for a candidate who is strong in first-principles thinking, demonstrates high user empathy, and can navigate technical conversations. They should be data-literate and capable of owning small-to-medium features from discovery to launch.

Case Study Prompt (for Product Sense interview):

Context: Helio is planning to introduce a new pricing tier in Q3 that limits access to certain advanced analytics features, currently available to all customers. Acme Co., a key customer, has expressed strong opposition to this change. 

Question: As the PM for the Lantern workspace, how would you analyze the potential impact of this change? What data would you look at? How would you frame the problem and potential solutions for leadership?

Evaluation Criteria:
- Problem Structuring: Can they break down the ambiguous problem?
- Data & Metrics: Do they identify relevant quantitative and qualitative inputs?
- Customer Empathy: Do they consider the user (Acme) perspective vs. just the business need?
- Tradeoffs: Can they articulate the pros and cons of different paths forward?

title: [Draft] Tradeoffs: PLT-880 Audit Log Migration in Q3
body:
To: Priya Iyer, Sarah Okafor
From: Alex Chen
Date: May 8, 2026

This document outlines the tradeoffs of incorporating the Platform team's audit log migration (PLT-880) into the Lantern team's Q3 roadmap.

**Background:**
The Platform team is deprecating the v1 audit log service and requires all teams to migrate to a new v2 API. They have requested this work be completed in Q3 to de-risk EOY deadlines. The initial estimate for the Lantern team is 4 engineering weeks.

**Option 1: Complete PLT-880 Migration in Q3**
*   **Pros:**
    *   Aligns with Platform team's timeline.
    *   Eliminates technical debt and future migration pressure in Q4.
    *   Stays on a supported, more secure service.
*   **Cons (Impact on Lantern Roadmap):**
    *   The 4-week estimate consumes ~30% of our Q3 backend engineering capacity.
    *   The Acme Co. performance dashboard project (LAN-2031), a key customer commitment, would be delayed until Q4.
    *   New Reporting Suite features would be descoped significantly.

**Option 2: Defer PLT-880 Migration to Q4**
*   **Pros:**
    *   Allows Lantern team to deliver on existing Q3 customer and business commitments (Acme dashboards, Reporting Suite).
    *   Provides more time to properly scope and resource the migration work.
*   **Cons:**
    *   Creates friction with the Platform team.
    *   Requires Lantern to operate on a deprecated service for an additional quarter, accepting the stated (though unquantified) security/stability risk.
    *   Adds pressure to an already busy Q4 roadmap.

**Recommendation:**
My recommendation is Option 2. The direct, committed revenue and customer satisfaction tied to the Acme Co. work outweighs the internal platform-driven deadline for PLT-880. We can formally slot the migration into the Q4 plan.

title: Tradeoffs for PLT-880 Audit Log work in Q3

Per our chat, here is the tradeoff analysis for pulling the PLT-880 work into Q3.

Doc link: [Draft] Tradeoffs: PLT-880 Audit Log Migration in Q3

My strong bias is to protect the Acme work and push this to Q4. Let me know what you think.

-Alex

title: Notes: Tech Sync PLT-880
body:
Date: 15 May 2026

**Attendees:**
* Sarah Okafor
* Mei Tanaka
* Tomas Reinholz
* Alex Chen

**Discussion:**
*   Tomas's team walked through the v2 API spec and the motivation for the changes (structured logging, better indexing for security searches).
*   Mei asked detailed questions about event batching and error handling, which seem to be the primary points of complexity for Lantern's implementation.
*   The current v1 client in Lantern has a lot of custom logic that would need to be re-thought, not just replaced.
*   Mei believes the core migration is straightforward, but building a robust, fault-tolerant implementation for Lantern's high-throughput event stream is the complex part.

**Outcome:**
*   The original 4-week estimate is likely too high for a basic migration, but too low for a truly robust one.
*   Mei will draft a short tech spec with two options: a 'quick-and-dirty' migration (~1.5 weeks) and a 'right way' migration (~3 weeks).

**Action Items:**
*   **AI-1 (Mei):** Draft 1-pager tech spec with two migration approaches and their respective risks/benefits. Due Mon, May 19.
